name: Team Objective
description: A shared objective tracks team progress, contributions, completion rules, and rewards under authoritative scoring.
design_notes: Team objectives need a server-owned objective_state_id, contribution tracking, team membership, late-join snapshots, and anti-exploit rules. Without those pieces, players can double-claim rewards, lose credit after disconnect, or see different completion states across clients.
design_purpose: Focus multiple players on a shared goal instead of separate personal tasks.
Make support, defense, gathering, escorting, or damage contributions visible and rewarding.
Create mission structure that survives disconnects, late joins, and changing team membership.
player_fantasy: Players feel they are pushing one objective forward together and that every contribution helps the team succeed.

parameters.name: team_id
parameters.description: Team identifier used for objective ownership, visibility, rewards, and permission checks.

parameters.name: objective_state_id
parameters.description: Authoritative state of the team objective.

parameters.name: contribution_log_id
parameters.description: Audit log of player_id contributions used for rewards, UI, and anti-exploit checks.

parameters.name: objective_points_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that converts player actions into objective progress.

parameters.name: late_join_snapshot_id
parameters.description: Snapshot payload used to initialize players who join after the objective has started.

parameters.name: anti_exploit_rule_id
parameters.description: Rule that prevents reward duplication, team switching abuse, or repeated contribution farming.

edge_cases: A player switches teams or disconnects after contributing but before rewards are distributed.
Two teams complete opposing objective conditions on the same server tick.
Late joiners enter after objective progress crossed a reward threshold.
Objective state changes while clients are still animating an earlier contested or failed state.
common_bugs: Objective completion is calculated on each client and produces split-brain success or failure states.
Contribution logs are reset on host migration and players lose credit.
Team rewards can be claimed twice by reconnecting with a new player record.
UI progress bars show predicted progress as committed progress and jump backward after correction.
balancing_notes: Objectives should reward non-damage roles such as defense, revives, resource delivery, and communication when those roles matter.
Contribution thresholds can prevent freeloading, but they should not punish support players whose impact is indirect.
Late join reward rules should be generous enough for social play and strict enough to prevent queue sniping.
accessibility_notes: Show objective status, contribution requirements, and failure reasons through text and persistent HUD elements.
Support ping and non-voice callouts for objective changes, especially when roles have different viewpoints.

implementation_notes.unity: Represent objectives as server-owned state machines with objective_state_id, team_id, contribution_log_id, and versioned progress. Clients may predict HUD animation, but rewards and completion should commit from a server event tied to player_id and team membership.
implementation_notes.godot: Keep objective state in an authoritative node or backend service and send snapshots to clients. RPC contribution requests should include player_id, team_id, objective version, and action metadata, then the server appends to the log or rejects stale updates.
implementation_notes.unreal: GameState or dedicated replicated actors can expose objective state, while GameMode owns scoring and reward commits. Use PlayerState for team membership and contribution credit so seamless travel and reconnect preserve identity.
implementation_notes.web: For browser co-op, keep objective evaluation on the server and send versioned snapshots with late_join_snapshot_id. Clients should display predicted progress separately until server_tick confirms the contribution.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses team objectives such as payloads and control points to structure matches.

example_games.title: Sea of Thieves
example_games.note: Uses crew-level goals and shared voyage progress in cooperative play.
